info.name: SecurePoll RW
info.description:
# SecurePoll RW — API collection

Auto-generated from the FastAPI OpenAPI spec — covers **every** endpoint, each with a usage description, auth note, and example body.

## Getting started
1. Set the `base_url` variable (default `http://127.0.0.1:8000`).
2. Run **auth > Login** — its test script captures `access_token` and `refresh_token` into collection variables automatically.
3. Every other request inherits Bearer auth (`{{access_token}}`) — just send it.

## Notes
- Public endpoints (no token): `POST /auth/login`, `/auth/token`, `/verifications`, `/votes`, `GET /health`.
- Path/query params are pre-filled with placeholder values — replace IDs with real ones (list endpoints return them).
- Each request's **Description** tab explains what it does and which params it takes.
- Seeded admin login: `admin@securepoll.rw` / `SecurePassword123!`.

request.description:
`GET /health` — Auth: public (no token)

Returns `{"status": "ok"}` when the API is reachable.  No authentication required.

item.name: auth
item.description: Authentication (JWT + TOTP 2FA), admin user management, RBAC roles, and session tracking.  Login flow: `POST /auth/login` → if `mfa_required`, `POST /auth/mfa` → full token.

request.description:
`POST /auth/login` — Auth: public (no token)

Authenticate with `email` + `password`.  

- If the account has MFA enabled, `mfa_required: true` is returned together with a short-lived partial token (5 min).  Pass that token to `POST /auth/mfa` with the TOTP code to upgrade to a full session token.
- If MFA is not configured, a full JWT is returned immediately.

Suspended accounts receive **403 Forbidden**.

`POST /auth/token` — Auth: public (no token)

OAuth2-compatible `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` login.  This endpoint is wired to the **Authorize** button in the Swagger UI — use `username` (email) and `password` fields.  Returns a full JWT (MFA is bypassed for the interactive docs flow).

`POST /auth/mfa` — Auth: public (no token)

Submit the 6-digit TOTP code from an authenticator app to upgrade a partial token (received from `POST /auth/login` when `mfa_required: true`) into a full session token.  

The partial token must be included in the `Authorization: Bearer …` header of this request.

`POST /auth/refresh` — Auth: public (no token)

Issue a fresh JWT for the authenticated user without requiring re-entry of credentials.  Useful for extending sessions before the current token expires.

`POST /auth/logout` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Signals logout intent.  Because JWTs are stateless, the token is not server-side invalidated — the client must discard the token.  To truly revoke access, use `POST /sessions/{session_id}:revoke`.

`GET /users` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns all admin user accounts in the system.  Requires an authenticated admin session.

item.name: Get the currently authenticated user's profile

request.description:
`GET /users/me` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the full profile of the user identified by the bearer token in the `Authorization` header.  Useful for UI profile pages and permission checks.

item.name: Get TOTP provisioning URI for QR code display

request.description:
`GET /users/{user_id}:totp-uri` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the TOTP provisioning URI and secret for the target user so a frontend can render a QR code for authenticator app setup.  

Requires that `POST /users/{user_id}:reset-mfa` has already been called to generate the secret.  Returns **400** if no secret exists yet.

**Path params:** `user_id`

item.name: Get a single admin user by ID

request.description:
`DELETE /users/{user_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Soft-deletes an admin user by suspending the account and writing a `USER_DELETED` audit entry.  The row is retained for audit chain integrity.  

**Constraints:**
- Cannot delete your own account.
- Cannot delete the last active super-admin (guard not implemented — use with care).

**Path params:** `user_id`

`POST /users:invite` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Create a new admin user account and set its status to `invitation_pending`.  

- `role_id` must reference an existing role.
- `email` must be unique — returns **400** if already registered.
- The invited user should change their password on first login.

item.name: Reset and re-provision TOTP secret for a user

request.description:
`POST /users/{user_id}:reset-mfa` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Generates a fresh TOTP secret for the target user, enables MFA on their account, and returns the raw secret for them to scan into an authenticator app (e.g., Google Authenticator, Authy).  

**Security note:** the secret is returned only once — store it securely or display as a QR code to the user immediately.

**Path params:** `user_id`

`POST /users/{user_id}:suspend` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Sets the target user's status to `suspended`, preventing them from logging in.  

**Constraint:** a user cannot suspend their own account — returns **400** if attempted.

**Path params:** `user_id`

`GET /roles` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns all roles defined in the system, each with an array of `permissions` strings.  Roles are referenced by `role_id` on admin user records.

`POST /roles` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Create a named role with an explicit list of permission strings.  Permission strings are arbitrary — the application checks for their presence in the role's `permissions` array.

`DELETE /roles/{role_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Hard-deletes a role by its string ID.  

Returns **400** if any admin users are currently assigned to this role — reassign those users to a different role before deleting.

**Path params:** `role_id`

item.name: List active sessions for current user

request.description:
`GET /sessions` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns all non-revoked sessions belonging to the currently authenticated user.  Each session record includes device, IP address, location, and last-active timestamp.

item.name: Activate a pending admin user account

request.description:
`POST /users/{user_id}:activate` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Set a user's status from `invitation_pending` to `active`, allowing them to log in.  

Typically called after an invited user has confirmed their email or an admin manually approves the account.  An audit entry is written.

**Path params:** `user_id`

item.name: Change the current user's own password

request.description:
`POST /auth/change-password` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Allows an authenticated user to change their own password by providing their current password for verification.  

- Returns **400** if `current_password` does not match the stored hash.
- The new password is immediately hashed and persisted.
- An audit entry is written recording the change (no passwords are logged).
**Query params:** `current_password`, `new_password`

item.name: geography
item.description: Districts and polling station management — reference geography data used throughout the voter registry and verification pipeline.

item.name: List all districts with optional province filter

request.description:
`GET /districts` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of districts.  

**Query parameters:**
- `province` — filter by `Province` enum value (`Kigali City`, `Northern`, `Southern`, `Eastern`, `Western`).
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination offset and page size (default 50).
**Query params:** `province`, `skip`, `limit`

`DELETE /districts/{district_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Permanently delete a district.  

Returns **400** if the district still has polling stations assigned to it — remove all stations first.  Returns **404** if the district does not exist.

**Path params:** `district_id`

item.name: List all polling stations with optional district filter

request.description:
`GET /polling-stations` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of polling stations.  

**Query parameters:**
- `district_id` — filter to one district's stations.
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination offset and page size (default 50).
**Query params:** `district_id`, `skip`, `limit`

item.name: Get a polling station by UUID

request.description:
`DELETE /polling-stations/{station_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Permanently delete a polling station.  

Returns **400** if the station still has voters registered to it — reassign them first.  Returns **404** if the station does not exist.

**Path params:** `station_id`

item.name: Open a polling station for voting

request.description:
`GET /polling-stations/{station_id}/summary` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a real-time summary for a polling station covering the current election day.  

**Fields returned:**
- `registered_count` — total voters assigned to this station.
- `verified_today` — biometric verifications completed today (from station counter).
- `votes_cast` — voters with status `voted` assigned to this station (queried live).
- `pending` — registered voters who have not yet voted.

Returns **404** if the station does not exist.

**Path params:** `station_id`

item.name: List all polling stations for a district
officers
item.description: Field officer management.  Officers are assigned to districts and conduct biometric enrolment and election-day verification.

item.name: List all field officers with optional district filter

request.description:
`GET /officers` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of field officers.  

**Query parameters:**
- `district_id` — filter to officers assigned to a specific district.
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination offset and page size (default 50).
**Query params:** `district_id`, `skip`, `limit`

item.name: Get a field officer by UUID

request.description:
`DELETE /officers/{officer_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Permanently remove a field officer record from the database.  

Field officers are not voters and carry no audit sensitivity, so a hard delete is permitted here.  Returns **204 No Content** on success and **404** if the officer does not exist.

**Path params:** `officer_id`

item.name: Get verification statistics for a field officer

request.description:
`GET /officers/{officer_id}/stats` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the total number of biometric verification attempts performed by this officer, broken down by result (`approved`, `manual_review`, `rejected`).  

Counts are queried live from the `verification_attempts` table.  Returns **404** if the officer does not exist.

**Path params:** `officer_id`

item.name: voters
item.description: Voter registry: create, search, update status, bulk CSV import/export.  Voters progress through statuses: `registered → voted`; side paths `blocked`, `flagged`, `archived`.

item.name: List voters with pagination and filtering

request.description:
`GET /voters` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of voters from the registry.  

**Query parameters:**
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination offset and page size (default 50).
- `search` — case-insensitive full-text search across `first_name`, `last_name`, `voter_token`, and `registration_ref`.
- `status` — filter by voter status (`registered`, `voted`, `blocked`, `flagged`, `archived`).
- `district` — filter by `district_id` UUID string.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`, `search`, `status`, `district`

item.name: Get a single voter by UUID

request.description:
`PATCH /voters/{voter_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Partially update a voter record (PATCH semantics — only supplied fields are changed).  

Updatable fields: `first_name`, `last_name`, `phone`, `roll_position`.  All changes are logged to the audit trail with a field-level diff.

**Path params:** `voter_id`

`POST /voters/{voter_id}:block` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Set the voter's status to `blocked` with a mandatory `reason` string.  A blocked voter cannot be verified or cast a vote.  Use `POST /voters/{voter_id}:restore` to reverse the block.

**Path params:** `voter_id`
**Query params:** `reason`, `note`

item.name: Restore a blocked or archived voter
Bulk-import voters from a CSV file

request.description:
`POST /voters:import` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Upload a UTF-8 CSV file to create multiple voter records in a single transaction.  

**Expected CSV columns** (header row required):  
`voter_token`, `registration_ref`, `national_id`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `sex` (`male`/`female`), `date_of_birth` (ISO 8601), `phone`, `district_id` (UUID), `polling_station_id` (UUID).

Rows that fail validation are counted as `rejected` and skipped — the rest are committed.

`GET /registry/health` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a quick summary of voter record counts broken down by status.  Useful as a dashboard widget to monitor registry data quality.

item.name: Flag a voter for manual review

request.description:
`POST /voters/{voter_id}:flag` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Set the voter's status to `flagged`, marking them for manual review.  A `reason` query parameter is required and logged to the audit trail.  A flagged voter can still be verified but is highlighted in reports.  Use `POST /voters/{voter_id}:restore` to clear the flag.

**Path params:** `voter_id`
**Query params:** `reason`

item.name: biometrics
item.description: Face biometric enrolment using ArcFace 512-d L2-normalized embeddings with AES-256-GCM encryption at rest and a passive liveness check.  A 1:N FAISS deduplication scan runs automatically at enrolment and creates fraud cases when cosine similarity exceeds the configured dedup threshold.

item.name: Re-enrol a voter's face biometric (replace existing template)

request.description:
`POST /biometrics/enroll` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Full biometric enrolment pipeline for a single voter:  

1. Decode the Base64 face image.
2. Run **ArcFace** inference to produce a 512-dimensional embedding.
3. Perform **passive liveness detection** — rejects spoofed/printed photos.
4. Encrypt the embedding with **AES-256-GCM** (random 96-bit nonce per template).
5. Add the embedding to the **FAISS** flat-L2 index and record the `faiss_id`.
6. Persist the `BiometricTemplate` row.
7. Run **1:N deduplication** — if any existing template has cosine similarity above the configured threshold, a `DuplicateMatch` and `FraudCase` are auto-created.

**Form fields:**  
- `voter_id` — UUID of the voter to enrol.
- `face_image` — Base64-encoded JPEG or PNG face image.

item.name: Get biometric template quality for a voter

request.description:
`GET /biometrics/quality/{voter_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the quality score, liveness result, and capture timestamp for the face biometric template enrolled for the given voter.  

**Quality score** is a proxy derived from the L2 norm of the ArcFace embedding (well-normalised embeddings score close to 1.0).  Returns **404** if no template has been enrolled yet.

**Path params:** `voter_id`

item.name: List all biometric templates with pagination

request.description:
`GET /biometrics/templates` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of all enrolled biometric templates across all voters.  

Each item includes the voter UUID, modality, quality score, liveness result, capture timestamp, FAISS index ID, and template UUID.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`

item.name: Delete the biometric template for a voter

request.description:
`DELETE /biometrics/templates/{voter_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Permanently removes the face biometric template enrolled for the given voter.  

**Warning:** this is a hard delete — the template cannot be recovered.  The voter will need to be re-enrolled before they can be biometrically verified.  An audit entry is written for every deletion.

**Path params:** `voter_id`

item.name: Re-run 1:N deduplication scan for a voter

request.description:
`POST /biometrics/dedup-scan/{voter_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Re-runs the 1:N FAISS deduplication scan for a voter's existing enrolled template.  

Retrieves the stored encrypted template, decrypts and re-embeds the raw bytes, then searches the FAISS index for near-duplicates above the configured `dedup_threshold`.  

Any hits that are not already recorded as `DuplicateMatch` records will be created, together with associated `FraudCase` entries.  Returns the list of duplicate hits found.

**Path params:** `voter_id`

`GET /biometrics/stats` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns enrollment coverage across the entire voter registry:  

- `total_voters` — all registered voters.
- `enrolled` — voters with at least one biometric template on file.
- `not_enrolled` — voters still pending biometric registration.
- `enrollment_rate` — percentage enrolled.
- `by_modality` — count of enrolled templates per biometric modality.

item.name: verification
item.description: Election-day 1:1 face verification and double-vote-protected vote casting.  The verify endpoint is public (no JWT) — field officers call it directly.  Vote casting uses a PostgreSQL row-lock to prevent concurrent double-votes.

request.description:
`POST /verifications` — Auth: public (no token)

Core election-day endpoint — verifies a voter's identity by comparing a live face image against the stored ArcFace template.  

**No JWT required** — this endpoint is called by field-officer devices using only a voter token and a live camera frame.

**Decision pipeline:**
1. Look up voter by `voter_token`.
2. Check eligibility (blocked/archived voters are rejected).
3. Retrieve and AES-256-GCM decrypt the stored embedding.
4. Embed the live face with ArcFace and compute cosine similarity.
5. Run liveness detection — liveness failure penalises confidence by −0.20.
6. Apply thresholds: `approved` ≥ face_match_threshold, `manual_review` ≥ review_floor, otherwise `rejected`.
7. Persist a `VerificationAttempt` row and write audit log.

Returns a `DecisionPanel` JSON with full explainability breakdown.

`GET /verifications` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Retrieve a paginated list of all verification attempts across all polling stations.  

**Query parameters:**
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination offset and page size (default 50).
- `station_id` — optional UUID to filter attempts by polling station.

Results are ordered newest-first.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`, `station_id`

item.name: Get a single verification attempt by ID
Cast a vote — atomically marks voter as voted

request.description:
`POST /votes` — Auth: public (no token)

Atomically marks a voter as `voted` using a PostgreSQL `SELECT … FOR UPDATE` row-lock.  

**Double-vote protection:**  
If two concurrent requests arrive for the same voter, the second blocks on the lock, then detects `status == voted` and returns **409 Conflict** — the vote is not counted twice.  

**Preconditions:**  
- Voter must exist.
- Status must be `registered` or `verified` — `blocked`, `flagged`, and `archived` are rejected.
- `status == voted` returns 409 (double-vote attempt, also logged to audit).

No JWT required — field officers call this directly after verification.

`POST /verifications/{attempt_id}:override` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Allows a supervisor to override the automated verification result of an attempt that was flagged for manual review.  

**Parameters:**
- `override_result` — new result string (`approved`, `rejected`, or `manual_review`).
- `reason` — mandatory justification text written to the audit log.

Sets `review_required` to `false` after override.

**Path params:** `attempt_id`
**Query params:** `override_result`, `reason`

item.name: Get verification log for a polling station

request.description:
`GET /verifications/station/{station_id}/log` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns all verification attempts recorded at a specific polling station, ordered newest-first, together with an aggregate summary (approved / manual_review / rejected counts).  

Useful for supervisors monitoring a station in real time.

**Path params:** `station_id`

item.name: fraud
item.description: Fraud cases, 1:N biometric duplicate matches, and real-time anomaly signal management.  Cases are auto-created by the biometric enrolment pipeline; operators can dismiss, escalate, or merge duplicate voter records.

request.description:
`GET /fraud/cases` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of all fraud cases in the system.  

Fraud cases are auto-created by the 1:N biometric deduplication pipeline at enrolment time, or can be raised manually by supervisors.  Use `skip` and `limit` for pagination.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`

item.name: Get a single fraud case by ID
Fraud case count summary by type

request.description:
`GET /fraud/summary` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the total number of fraud cases and a breakdown by `FraudType`.  Useful as a top-level dashboard metric.

item.name: List duplicate biometric matches with pagination

request.description:
`GET /duplicates` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of `DuplicateMatch` records produced by the 1:N FAISS deduplication scan run at enrolment time.  

Filter by `status` to see only pending, merged, or dismissed matches.  Each match links two voter IDs with a cosine similarity score.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`, `status`

item.name: Get a single duplicate match by ID

request.description:
`POST /duplicates/{match_id}:merge` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Resolve a duplicate match by designating one voter record as the **survivor** and archiving the other (the loser).  

**Steps:**
1. Identify loser: the voter in the match that is **not** `survivor_id`.
2. Set loser's status to `archived`.
3. Mark the `DuplicateMatch` as `merged` and record resolution metadata.
4. Write an audit entry.

The `survivor_id` must be one of the two voter IDs in the match.

**Path params:** `match_id`

`GET /anomalies` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns a paginated list of real-time anomaly signals detected by monitoring rules.  

Signals include statistical deviations such as unexpected turnout spikes, rejection-rate anomalies, or unusually high duplicate rates.  Filter by `status` (e.g. `active`, `acknowledged`, `muted`).
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`

item.name: Get a single anomaly signal by ID
audit
item.description: Append-only SHA-256 hash-chained audit log.  Every write operation across the system produces an immutable audit entry.  The `POST /audit:verify-chain` endpoint walks the full chain and reports any tampered rows.

item.name: List audit log entries with filtering

request.description:
`GET /audit/entries` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Browse the append-only audit log.  Results are ordered newest-first (by sequence number).  

**Query parameters:**
- `skip` / `limit` — pagination (default 100 per page).
- `action` — filter by `AuditAction` enum value (e.g. `LOGIN`, `VOTER_VERIFIED`).
- `actor_id` — filter by the UUID or string ID of the actor.
- `service` — filter by originating service name (e.g. `Auth`, `Verification`).

Every write operation across the system (login, enrolment, vote, override, etc.) produces an immutable entry with a SHA-256 hash chaining it to the previous entry.
**Query params:** `skip`, `limit`, `action`, `actor_id`, `service`

item.name: Get a single audit entry by UUID

request.description:
`POST /audit:verify-chain` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Walk every entry in the audit log in sequence order and recompute each SHA-256 hash, checking it against the stored `entry_hash`.  

**Tamper detection:**  If any database row has been modified (via direct SQL UPDATE or DELETE), the recomputed hash will differ from the stored value and a *break* is recorded.  

Returns:
- `entries_walked` — total rows checked.
- `breaks_found` — number of hash mismatches.
- `first_break_sequence` — sequence number of the earliest tampered entry (`null` if clean).
- `duration_ms` — time taken for the full walk.

This is a **read-only** operation — it does not modify any data.

`GET /audit:export` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Download the complete audit log as a CSV file ordered by sequence number (oldest first).  

The CSV includes all fields needed for offline chain verification:  
`sequence`, `occurred_at`, `action`, `actor_type`, `actor_id`, `actor_role`, `service`, `detail`, `entry_hash`, `prev_hash`.
**Query params:** `format`

item.name: analytics
item.description: Real-time turnout statistics, demographic breakdown by sex and district, and verification performance metrics (approval rate, average confidence).

request.description:
`GET /analytics/turnout` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the current election-day turnout figures:  

- `total_registered` — number of voters in the registry.
- `total_verified` — voters who have been biometrically verified at least once today.
- `total_voted` — voters whose status is `voted`.
- `turnout_rate` — percentage of registered voters who have voted.
- `by_station` — per-polling-station breakdown (registered, voted, turnout %).

All figures are computed in real time from the database.

`GET /analytics/demographics` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns demographic distribution of the voter registry:  

- `by_sex` — count of `male` and `female` voters.
- `by_district` — per-district registered voter count.

These figures reflect the registry at query time (not election-day snapshots).

`GET /analytics/live` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Combined real-time dashboard aggregating turnout, verification performance, and active anomaly count in a single response.  Intended for the supervisor dashboard displayed on election day.  All figures are computed live.

`GET /analytics/verification` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns aggregate statistics across all verification attempts:  

- `total_attempts` — total number of verification calls made.
- `approved` / `manual_review` / `rejected` — counts by decision outcome.
- `average_confidence` — mean confidence score across all attempts (0.0 – 1.0).
- `approval_rate` — percentage of attempts that resulted in `approved`.

Note: these counts include all attempts since system start, not just today.

`GET /analytics/enrollment` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns enrollment coverage across the voter registry:  

- `total_voters` — total voters registered.
- `enrolled` — voters with at least one biometric template.
- `not_enrolled` — voters pending biometric registration.
- `enrollment_rate` — percentage enrolled.
- `by_district` — per-district enrolled count and rate.

`GET /analytics/fraud` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns aggregate fraud metrics:  

- `total_cases` — all fraud cases ever raised.
- `open_cases` / `resolved_cases` — case status breakdown.
- `by_type` — count per `FraudType`.
- `by_risk_level` — count per `RiskLevel`.
- `total_duplicates` — duplicate biometric matches.
- `active_anomalies` — live anomaly signals.

item.name: keys
item.description: AES-256-GCM encryption key registry.  Each key record tracks its current version for rotation auditing.  The `POST /keys/{id}:rotate` endpoint increments the version and writes a `KEY_ROTATED` audit entry.  `GET /keys/health` simulates an HSM health probe.

item.name: HSM health check — list all key versions and algorithms
Register a new encryption key record

request.description:
`POST /keys` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Create a metadata record for a new encryption key.  

**Fields:**
- `title` — human-readable label (e.g. `Biometric Template Key 2025`).
- `algorithm` — cipher algorithm (default `AES-256-GCM`).
- `scope` — optional scope string (e.g. `biometrics`, `backups`).

The key is created at `current_version = 1`.  Call `:rotate` to increment the version when key material is rotated in the HSM.

item.name: Get an encryption key record by UUID

request.description:
`PATCH /keys/{key_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Partially update the metadata of an encryption key record (PATCH semantics).  

Mutable fields: `title`, `scope`.  Omitted fields are left unchanged.  Returns **404** if the key does not exist.

**Path params:** `key_id`

`DELETE /keys/{key_id}` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Permanently remove an encryption key metadata record.  

**Warning:** this does not rotate or destroy the underlying key material in the HSM — it only removes the tracking record.  An audit entry with action `RECORD_DELETED` is written before deletion.  Returns **204 No Content** on success.

**Path params:** `key_id`

item.name: Rotate an encryption key — increment its version

request.description:
`POST /keys/{key_id}:rotate` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Signal that the underlying key material for this record has been rotated in the HSM.  

**Effect:**
- Increments `current_version` by 1.
- Updates `updated_at` to the current UTC timestamp.
- Writes an audit entry with action `KEY_ROTATED`.

Returns **404** if the key record does not exist.

**Path params:** `key_id`

item.name: ai
item.description: AI/ML model health, FAISS index status, threshold tuning, and index rebuild.  `GET /ai/status` reports the active backend and FAISS size; `PUT /ai/thresholds` retunes match/review/dedup at runtime; `POST /ai/rebuild-index` reconstructs the 1:N index from the encrypted templates (repairing any drift).

item.name: AI model and FAISS index status

request.description:
`GET /ai/status` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the current status of the AI/ML subsystem:  

- `model_loaded` — whether the ArcFace face embedding model is loaded in memory.
- `faiss_index_size` — number of embeddings currently indexed in FAISS.
- `face_match_threshold` — minimum cosine similarity to approve a verification.
- `review_floor` — minimum confidence to flag for manual review instead of reject.
- `dedup_threshold` — similarity above which two templates are considered duplicates.
- `faiss_index_path` — path to the persisted FAISS index file on disk.

Does not require authentication — safe to call from health-check scripts.

`GET /ai/thresholds` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Returns the three thresholds that drive the biometric decision pipeline:  

- `face_match_threshold` — cosine similarity required for an `approved` decision.
- `review_floor` — lower bound for `manual_review` (below → `rejected`).
- `dedup_threshold` — similarity at which two enrolled templates are flagged as duplicates.

All thresholds are sourced from application settings and can be overridden via environment variables.

item.name: Update AI decision thresholds at runtime

request.description:
`PUT /ai/thresholds` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Override one or more biometric decision thresholds for the current server process.  

**Fields (all optional):**
- `face_match_threshold` — minimum cosine similarity for an `approved` decision (0.0–1.0).
- `review_floor` — lower bound for `manual_review`; scores below this are `rejected` (0.0–1.0).
- `dedup_threshold` — similarity at which two templates are flagged as duplicates (0.0–1.0).

**Warning:** changes are applied to the in-process `settings` object and take effect immediately but are **not persisted** — they reset to environment-variable defaults on server restart.  To persist thresholds, update the `.env` file or environment variables.  

An audit entry is written recording old and new values.

item.name: Rebuild FAISS index from stored templates
Ping AI model and report health

request.description:
`POST /ai/healthcheck` — Auth: Bearer {{access_token}}

Performs a lightweight health check of the AI/ML subsystem and writes an audit entry.  

Returns:  
- `status` — `ok` if the face model is loaded and FAISS index is accessible; `degraded` otherwise.
- `model_loaded` — whether the ArcFace model is in memory.
- `index_size` — number of vectors in the FAISS index.
- `checked_at` — ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the check was performed.
